title: Define frame types, frame format, error taxonomy, and codec
purpose: Establishes the protocol foundation: the frame type enumeration covering all bus primitives, the fixed-size binary header format, typed frame body variants, the error taxonomy with numeric reason codes, and the synchronous zero-copy codec for encoding and decoding frames. This is the single most critical brief in the protocol cluster — every other brief builds frame types and codec extensions on this foundation.
task: Create the protocol module skeleton and its four foundational files. Define FrameType with all 20 variants covering lifecycle, conversation, subscription, publish, backpressure, and keepalive categories. Define the fixed-size frame header (type u8, flags u8, stream_id u32, payload_length u32 — 10 bytes total). Define typed Frame body variants with fields matching their category. Enforce stream 0 for control frames and stream >= 1 for application frames at construction time. Define ProtocolError with numeric reason codes for every error condition. Implement the codec: encode writes frames to byte buffers as header + payload; decode reads frames from byte buffers returning the frame and bytes consumed; decode returns ProtocolError for malformed input; decode skips unknown frame types by reading their length prefix. Verify the round-trip property: decode(encode(frame)) == frame for every frame type. Scope boundary: this brief defines the format and codec. It does not implement envelope semantics (PROTO-002), connection lifecycle (PROTO-003), multiplexing logic (PROTO-004), backpressure state (PROTO-005), or schema negotiation (PROTO-006).

requirements.title: Wire protocol module with pub mod declarations only
requirements.spec: protocol/mod.rs SHALL contain only pub mod declarations for frame, codec, error, and future submodules (envelope, causal, handshake, lifecycle, version, multiplex, backpressure, schema), plus pub use re-exports for core public types. mod.rs SHALL NOT contain any logic, type definitions, or function bodies.
acceptance: mod.rs contains pub mod declarations for all protocol submodules.
mod.rs contains pub use re-exports for FrameType, Frame, FrameHeader, ProtocolError, and codec functions.
mod.rs contains zero function bodies, zero struct/enum definitions.
The module compiles with `use crate::protocol::{Frame, FrameType, ProtocolError};`.

files: docs/design/protocol/design.json:33-45 — canonical protocol module structure; includes `mod.rs`, `frame.rs`, `codec.rs`, `error.rs`, and future submodules to declare now.
docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:14-22 — exact R1 acceptance for declarations, re-exports, and `use crate::protocol::{Frame, FrameType, ProtocolError};`.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-8 — crate root currently exposes `causal`, `channel`, `conversation`, `envelope`, and `error`; add `pub mod protocol;` or the R1 import cannot compile.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5 — existing module-root convention: `pub mod` declarations followed by `pub use` re-exports only.
CONVENTIONS.toml:74-91 — entrypoint/module files have tighter 200-line advisory limit and Rust lint activation; keep `protocol/mod.rs` tiny.
context: The repository already follows CN3 in `channel/mod.rs`: no function bodies or type definitions, just declarations and re-exports.
The protocol directory is absent, so create the whole `crates/liminal/src/protocol/` tree and remember the crate-root declaration in `src/lib.rs` even though the brief's file list omits it.

R1 requires future submodule declarations (`envelope`, `causal`, `handshake`, `lifecycle`, `version`, `multiplex`, `backpressure`, `schema`) before their implementations exist; Rust will require stub files for each declared module unless you defer declarations, so plan to create empty stub files or coordinate with the brief's intent.
Core public re-exports should include `Frame`, `FrameHeader`, `FrameType`, `ProtocolError`, and codec functions such as `encode`/`decode` from `protocol/mod.rs`.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs` as a declaration-only module root: `pub mod frame;`, `pub mod codec;`, `pub mod error;`, plus the future submodule declarations the brief names, then `pub use` the core public API from `frame`, `error`, and `codec`. Add `pub mod protocol;` to `crates/liminal/src/lib.rs` so `crate::protocol::{Frame, FrameType, ProtocolError}` resolves. If declaring future submodules now, create minimal empty files for them; keep all logic in `frame.rs`, `codec.rs`, and `error.rs`, not in `mod.rs`.
scout.notes: The biggest trap is that Rust module declarations are not just documentation: every `pub mod envelope;` needs a corresponding `protocol/envelope.rs` or `protocol/envelope/mod.rs`. Empty stubs preserve the R1 shape without implementing PROTO-002 through PROTO-006; do not move root-level `src/envelope.rs` or `src/causal.rs` into the protocol module as part of this brief.

requirements.title: Define ProtocolError with numeric reason codes
requirements.spec: ProtocolError enum SHALL include variants for: malformed frame (incomplete header, truncated payload), unknown frame type (for logging, not disconnection), invalid stream (control frame on non-zero stream, application frame on stream 0), invalid state transition, authentication failure, version mismatch, schema incompatible, and codec errors. Each variant SHALL carry a numeric reason code (u16) and an optional human-readable message. ProtocolError SHALL implement std::error::Error via thiserror.
acceptance: ProtocolError has variants covering all error conditions listed in CN10.
Each variant carries or maps to a unique numeric reason code (u16).
ProtocolError::reason_code() returns the u16 code.
ProtocolError::message() returns an optional human-readable string.
ProtocolError implements std::error::Error and Debug.

files: docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:31-49 — ProtocolError variants, reason code API, message API, and S11 acceptance.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:48-58 — constraints CN2, CN4, CN6, CN9, CN10: no panics, Debug/Error, malformed bytes become ProtocolError, unknown types are skippable, no silent drops.
docs/design/protocol/stories.json:27-33 — operator story S11: numeric reason codes must support monitoring without parsing logs.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — existing `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]` style for crate errors.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-16 — `thiserror = "2"` is already available and crate lints inherit the workspace.
context: Existing error taxonomy derives `Debug` and `thiserror::Error`; match that style for `ProtocolError` rather than hand-writing `std::error::Error`.

The brief names specific malformed-frame cases that codec tests will likely assert by variant: `IncompleteHeader` and `TruncatedPayload` should be separate variants, not collapsed into a generic malformed error.
Unknown frame type is an error-taxonomy variant for logging/metrics, but R6 says decoding unknown frame types must not return that error; do not wire `FrameType::Unknown` to a disconnect/error path.
Prefer fixed variant-to-code mapping in `reason_code()` over storing arbitrary user-provided codes, so uniqueness cannot be violated at construction time.
scout.approach: Implement `ProtocolError` in `protocol/error.rs` with variants for `IncompleteHeader`, `TruncatedPayload`, `UnknownFrameType`, `InvalidStream`, `InvalidStateTransition`, `AuthenticationFailure`, `VersionMismatch`, `SchemaIncompatible`, and `CodecError`, each carrying `message: Option<String>` where helpful. Add `reason_code(&self) -> u16` with stable unique constants and `message(&self) -> Option<&str>` to avoid cloning. Use `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error, PartialEq, Eq)]` if tests will compare errors directly; if deriving equality becomes awkward, tests can compare variants via `matches!` and reason codes.
scout.notes: This crate's clippy configuration denies unwrap/expect/panic workspace-wide, so even error formatting helpers and tests should avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()`. Keep the taxonomy protocol-specific; do not merge it into the existing top-level `LiminalError` unless a later integration brief asks for that.

files_changed.note: ProtocolError enum with stable u16 reason codes and optional messages.

dev.how: Defined ProtocolError with variants for incomplete headers, truncated payloads, unknown frame types, invalid streams, invalid state transitions, authentication failures, version mismatches, schema incompatibility, and codec errors. Each variant maps to a unique stable u16 via reason_code(), exposes message(), derives Debug, and implements std::error::Error via thiserror.

checklist.note: All required protocol error categories are represented and reason_code()/message() APIs are implemented.

stories.note: Every protocol error variant has a numeric reason code suitable for explicit error frames and monitoring.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolError has variants covering all error conditions listed in CN10.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:5-48` defines variants for incomplete header, truncated payload, unknown frame type, invalid stream, invalid state transition, authentication failure, version mismatch, schema incompatible, and codec error.

acceptance.criterion: Each variant carries or maps to a unique numeric reason code (u16).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:52-68` defines unique u16 reason constants `0x0001` through `0x0009`; `reason_code()` maps every variant at lines 72-84.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolError::reason_code() returns the u16 code.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:72-84` implements `pub const fn reason_code(&self) -> u16` with every variant matched.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolError::message() returns an optional human-readable string.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:88-99` returns `message.as_deref()` for every variant.

requirements.title: Define FrameType enum with all 20 variants
requirements.spec: FrameType enum SHALL have variants: Connect, ConnectAck, ConnectError, Disconnect, Subscribe, SubscribeAck, SubscribeError, Unsubscribe, Publish, PublishAck, PublishError, ConversationOpen, ConversationMessage, ConversationClose, ConversationError, Accept, Defer, Reject, Ping, Pong. Each variant SHALL map to a unique u8 discriminant. FrameType SHALL implement Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, and conversion to/from u8. Unknown u8 values SHALL map to a FrameType::Unknown(u8) variant for forward compatibility.
acceptance: FrameType has exactly 20 named variants plus Unknown(u8).
Each named variant has a unique u8 discriminant.
FrameType::from(u8) returns the correct variant for known values and Unknown(n) for unknown values.
u8::from(FrameType) returns the discriminant.
FrameType implements Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq.

files: docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:50-68 — exact 20 named `FrameType` variants plus `Unknown(u8)` and conversion requirements.
docs/design/protocol/checklist.json:12-18 — checklist C4/C6 confirms the full frame category list and Debug requirement.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:6-14 — design rationale for lifecycle, subscription, publish, conversation, backpressure, keepalive, stream IDs, and additive versioning.
docs/design/decisions.json:140-153 — ADR-008: custom binary protocol and protocol versioning must be designed in from the start.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:4-18 — existing derive style for public enums/structs: `Clone`, `Copy`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, `Eq` where applicable.
context: `FrameType` should derive `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`; `Unknown(u8)` can still be `Copy` because it stores only a byte.
Do not rely on `as u8` casts for an enum with `Unknown(u8)`; implement `From<u8> for FrameType` and `From<FrameType> for u8` manually so `Unknown(n)` round-trips to `n`.
Pick stable discriminants now and treat them as SDK-facing wire ABI; changing them later breaks cross-language clients.
Additive versioning means unknown values must survive parsing as `FrameType::Unknown(n)` rather than causing decode failure.
scout.approach: Define `FrameType` in `protocol/frame.rs` with the 20 unit variants in a documented, stable order and explicit constants in the `From` impls. A simple discriminant plan is `0x01..=0x14` for the 20 named variants, reserving `0x00` for unknown/invalid future use if desired; whatever mapping is chosen, keep it centralized in the conversion match arms. Add focused unit tests for known values, all unique discriminants, and an unknown value mapping to `Unknown(n)`.
scout.notes: Because `FrameType::Unknown(u8)` is a real public variant, `u8::from(FrameType::Unknown(n))` should return `n`, not `0` or a sentinel. This matters for R6 unknown-frame skip and for packet-capture/debug tooling.

files_changed.note: FrameType enum with 20 named variants plus Unknown(u8).

dev.how: Added FrameType with the exact 20 named protocol categories, Unknown(u8), stable discriminants 0x01 through 0x14, and manual From<u8>/From<FrameType> for forward-compatible round trips.

checklist.note: FrameType covers Connect, ConnectAck, ConnectError, Disconnect, Subscribe, SubscribeAck, SubscribeError, Unsubscribe, Publish, PublishAck, PublishError, ConversationOpen, ConversationMessage, ConversationClose, ConversationError, Accept, Defer, Reject, Ping, and Pong.
FrameType and Frame derive Debug; FrameHeader also derives Debug.

acceptance.criterion: FrameType has exactly 20 named variants plus Unknown(u8).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:8-50` lists the 20 named variants `Connect` through `Pong` plus `Unknown(u8)`.

acceptance.criterion: Each named variant has a unique u8 discriminant.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:69-94` maps wire values `0x01..0x14` uniquely to named variants; `protocol::frame::tests::frame_type_discriminants_round_trip` passed.

acceptance.criterion: FrameType::from(u8) returns the correct variant for known values and Unknown(n) for unknown values.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:69-94` implements `From<u8>` with `unknown => Self::Unknown(unknown)`; test assertions at `frame.rs:421-450` passed.

acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:97-123` implements `From<FrameType> for u8`; `frame_type_discriminants_round_trip` passed.

requirements.title: Define frame header and typed frame body variants
requirements.spec: FrameHeader struct SHALL contain: frame_type (FrameType), flags (u8), stream_id (u32), payload_length (u32). The header is fixed at 10 bytes on the wire. Frame enum SHALL have one variant per FrameType, each carrying typed fields appropriate to that frame category (not raw bytes). WHEN constructing a frame, THE SYSTEM SHALL validate stream ID: control frames (Connect, ConnectAck, ConnectError, Disconnect, Ping, Pong) SHALL require stream_id == 0; application frames SHALL require stream_id >= 1. Construction with an invalid stream ID SHALL return ProtocolError::InvalidStream.
acceptance: FrameHeader is exactly 10 bytes when serialized (1 + 1 + 4 + 4).
Frame has one variant per FrameType with typed fields.
Frame::new_ping(stream_id=1) returns Err(ProtocolError::InvalidStream).
Frame::new_ping(stream_id=0) returns Ok(Frame::Ping).
Frame::new_publish(stream_id=0, ..) returns Err(ProtocolError::InvalidStream).
All Frame variants and FrameHeader implement Debug.

files: docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:69-88 — frame header fields, 10-byte wire size, typed `Frame` body variants, and stream ID constructor invariants.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:6-14 — design-level frame categories, fixed header, stream 0 reservation, in-band backpressure, versioning, and explicit errors.
docs/design/protocol/checklist.json:12-18 — checklist C3/C5/C7 for fixed binary prefix, typed frame bodies, and stream validation.
docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-002.json:1-11 — next brief owns MessageEnvelope/CausalContext semantics; PROTO-001 should not overbuild them.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-34 — existing convention for public structs with documented fields and simple constructors.
context: Header serialization must be exactly 10 bytes: frame type `u8`, flags `u8`, stream ID `u32`, payload length `u32`; do not use Rust struct layout or `size_of::<FrameHeader>()` as the wire size.

Control frames are `Connect`, `ConnectAck`, `ConnectError`, `Disconnect`, `Ping`, and `Pong`; all others, including `Accept`, `Defer`, and `Reject`, are application-stream frames and must reject stream 0.
R6 requires an unknown decoded frame representation, so `Frame` should include `Unknown { type_id, flags, stream_id, payload }` in addition to one variant per named frame type.

Typed fields are required, but envelope/causal/handshake/schema semantics are explicitly later briefs; keep fields structural and losslessly serializable without implementing state machines or validation beyond stream ID.
scout.approach: Define `FrameHeader` as a public Rust struct plus a `pub const HEADER_LEN: usize = 10`. Define `Frame` with named variants for all frame types, carrying enough typed data to serialize deterministically: stream/flags metadata, reason-code/message fields for error variants, identifiers or byte/string fields for publish/subscribe/conversation placeholders, and `Vec<u8>` only where the field is truly opaque payload pending PROTO-002. Implement smart constructors such as `new_ping(stream_id)`, `new_pong`, `new_publish`, and a common `validate_stream(frame_type, stream_id)` helper returning `ProtocolError::InvalidStream` rather than panicking.
scout.notes: The brief says each Frame variant should hold typed fields, but it does not fully specify each payload layout. Save dev time by choosing a small deterministic internal layout now and documenting it in code comments/tests; avoid pulling in root `src/envelope.rs` or designing causal/schema semantics that PROTO-002/006 own.

files_changed.note: FrameHeader, typed Frame enum, stream validation helper, and smart constructors.

dev.how: Defined FrameHeader with frame_type, flags, stream_id, and payload_length plus a 10-byte wire length constant. Defined one typed Frame variant per named FrameType plus Unknown for forward compatibility, and enforced stream 0 for control frames versus non-zero application streams through validate_stream(), Frame::new_ping(), Frame::new_publish(), and decode validation.

checklist.note: Header serialization is fixed at 10 bytes: u8 type, u8 flags, u32 stream ID, u32 payload length.
Named Frame variants carry typed structural fields; opaque Vec<u8> is used only for payload bytes or unknown-frame preservation.
Frame construction and known-frame decoding validate stream 0/control and stream >=1/application invariants.

stories.note: The fixed-size header lets callers read exactly 10 bytes to determine type, flags, stream ID, and payload length.

acceptance.criterion: FrameHeader is exactly 10 bytes when serialized (1 + 1 + 4 + 4).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:4` sets `HEADER_LEN = 10`; `frame.rs:125-140` defines the header fields; `protocol::codec::tests::encode_writes_header_fields_in_wire_order` passed and asserts byte offsets at `codec/tests.rs:37-41`.

acceptance.criterion: Frame has one variant per FrameType with typed fields.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:143-268` defines typed `Frame` variants for every named `FrameType` plus `Unknown`; `frame_type()` maps them at `frame.rs:301-327`.

acceptance.criterion: Frame::new_ping(stream_id=1) returns Err(ProtocolError::InvalidStream).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:277-280` validates ping streams; `protocol::frame::tests::constructors_validate_streams` asserts the error at `frame.rs:456-459`.

acceptance.criterion: Frame::new_ping(stream_id=0) returns Ok(Frame::Ping).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:277-280` returns `Ok(Self::Ping { flags: 0 })`; `constructors_validate_streams` asserts success at `frame.rs:455`.

acceptance.criterion: Frame::new_publish(stream_id=0, ..) returns Err(ProtocolError::InvalidStream).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:287-299` validates publish streams; `constructors_validate_streams` asserts the stream-0 error at `frame.rs:461-464`.

acceptance.criterion: All Frame variants and FrameHeader implement Debug.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:126` derives `Debug` for `FrameHeader`; `frame.rs:144` derives `Debug` for `Frame`.

issues: API-hardening caveat from independent review: because `Frame` is a public enum with struct-like variants, downstream code can directly instantiate an invalid stream ID such as `Frame::Publish { stream_id: 0, .. }` before `encode()` rejects it via `frame.validate()?`. This does not fail the explicit R4 acceptance criteria, which require the named `new_ping`/`new_publish` constructor checks and codec validation, but it is a stricter interpretation of the spec wording "WHEN constructing a frame" to consider for a future sealed/constructor-only API pass.

requirements.title: Implement codec encode writing frame to byte buffer
requirements.spec: encode(frame, buffer) SHALL write the frame to a mutable byte buffer as: 10-byte header followed by the serialized payload. The payload_length field in the header SHALL match the actual serialized payload length. encode SHALL operate synchronously on byte slices (P4). encode SHALL be infallible for valid frames — a successfully constructed Frame always encodes (CN6).
acceptance: encode writes exactly header_size + payload_length bytes.
The first byte is the frame type discriminant.
Bytes 1 is flags, 2..6 is stream_id (big-endian u32), 6..10 is payload_length (big-endian u32).
encode returns the number of bytes written.
encode does not allocate — it writes to the provided buffer.

files: docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:89-107 — encode acceptance: header + payload, big-endian offsets, return byte count, no allocation.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:6-14 — P2/P4/P7 framing principles: self-describing, length-delimited, synchronous byte-buffer codec.
docs/design/protocol/checklist.json:21-29 — checklist C8-C12 establishes codec scope and round-trip/unknown-skip neighbors.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-8 — codec functions re-exported from `protocol/mod.rs` must become reachable through the crate module tree.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace clippy denies unsafe code, unwrap, expect, and panic; byte writes must be bounds-checked.
context: Acceptance fixes byte positions: byte 0 type, byte 1 flags, bytes 2..6 stream ID big-endian, bytes 6..10 payload length big-endian.
`encode` must not allocate; compute payload length first and write directly into the caller-provided `&mut [u8]` or equivalent buffer.

A valid frame should not fail due to its own contents, but a too-small output buffer is still an environmental error; expose an `encoded_len(frame)` helper or return `ProtocolError::CodecError` for insufficient capacity without panicking.
Frame payload serialization should live beside the codec or behind methods on `Frame`; do not duplicate length calculations and actual writes because that creates header/payload length drift.
scout.approach: Create `protocol/codec.rs` with `pub fn encode(frame: &Frame, buffer: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, ProtocolError>` unless the team standardizes on a different signature; make the happy path write exactly `HEADER_LEN + payload_len`. Implement helpers like `payload_len(frame)`, `write_header`, and `write_payload` that use checked `get_mut` ranges and `to_be_bytes()`. If the brief's tests expect a bare `usize`, consider wrapping the fallible lower-level function with a preconditioned API plus `encoded_len`, but do not panic on small buffers.
scout.notes: The wording 'encode is infallible for valid frames' conflicts slightly with 'writes to provided buffer' when the buffer is too small. Resolve that in code comments and tests: frame validity is infallible, buffer capacity is a caller error reported as `ProtocolError::CodecError`.

files_changed.note: Public encode() and encoded_len() plus frame payload length/write dispatch.
Private checked payload writer and length helpers extracted to keep files under 500 lines.

dev.how: Implemented encode(&Frame, &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, ProtocolError> to write the fixed header and serialized payload into the caller-provided buffer, using big-endian u32 fields at the specified offsets and returning the exact byte count. Valid frames encode deterministically; environmental buffer capacity errors are explicit CodecError results rather than panics.
dev.deviation: Extracted private payload writer/length helpers into crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs to satisfy CN1's 500-line file limit; the public codec API and behavior follow the scouted approach.

checklist.note: encode() writes header + payload with a payload_length matching the serialized payload and no allocation in the encode path.

stories.note: Encoding is deterministic and covered by round-trip and header-layout tests.

acceptance.criterion: encode writes exactly header_size + payload_length bytes.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:37-65` returns `total_len`; `protocol::codec::tests::round_trips_all_named_frame_types` asserts `written == len` at `codec/tests.rs:7-10`; `cargo test --workspace` passed.

acceptance.criterion: The first byte is the frame type discriminant.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:121-132` writes `u8::from(frame.frame_type())` first; `codec/tests.rs:37-38` asserts the first byte for a Publish frame.

acceptance.criterion: Bytes 1 is flags, 2..6 is stream_id (big-endian u32), 6..10 is payload_length (big-endian u32).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:126-131` writes type, flags, stream id, and payload length in order through big-endian writer methods; `codec/tests.rs:39-41` asserts bytes 1, 2..6, and 6..10.

acceptance.criterion: encode returns the number of bytes written.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:64` returns `Ok(total_len)`; tests assert the returned value at `codec/tests.rs:9-10`, `32-37`, and `82-84`.

acceptance.criterion: encode does not allocate — it writes to the provided buffer.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:37-65` accepts `&mut [u8]`, slices it at lines 50-62, and writes through `PayloadWriter`; `payload.rs:73-84` copies directly into the provided slice.

issues: Valid `Frame::ConversationClose { reason_code: None, .. }` previously over-counted the optional u16 field in `encoded_payload_len`, causing `write_payload` to leave unwritten bytes and `encode()` to fail with a codec error; this contradicted CN6/R5's valid-frame infallibility claim.
fixes: Changed `option_u16_len` to depend on the actual `Option<u16>` value (`crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs:43-48`) and changed `encoded_payload_len` to pass `*reason_code` (`codec.rs:165-173`).

requirements.title: Implement codec decode with error handling and unknown-type skip
requirements.spec: decode(buffer) SHALL read a frame from a byte buffer, returning the decoded Frame and the number of bytes consumed, or a ProtocolError. WHEN the buffer contains fewer than 10 bytes, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ProtocolError::IncompleteHeader. WHEN the buffer contains a complete header but insufficient payload bytes, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ProtocolError::TruncatedPayload. WHEN the frame type is unknown, THE SYSTEM SHALL skip the frame by reading payload_length bytes and returning a Frame::Unknown { type_id, payload } variant — it SHALL NOT disconnect or error (CN9). decode SHALL NEVER panic (CN6).
acceptance: decode of a valid encoded frame returns Ok((frame, bytes_consumed)).
decode of a buffer shorter than 10 bytes returns ProtocolError with IncompleteHeader.
decode of a buffer with complete header but truncated payload returns ProtocolError with TruncatedPayload.
decode of an unknown frame type returns Frame::Unknown with the raw payload, consuming header + payload bytes.
decode never panics for any input (fuzz-safe).

files: docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:108-126 — decode acceptance for incomplete header, truncated payload, unknown type, bytes consumed, and fuzz safety.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:48-58 — CN6/CN9 require malformed bytes to return ProtocolError and unknown types to be skipped via length prefix.
docs/design/protocol/stories.json:5-12 — S1/S2/S3 client-SDK stories for fixed header, skippable unknown frames, and deterministic codec behavior.
docs/design/protocol/checklist.json:21-29 — C9/C10/C12 are the decode-specific checklist items.
Cargo.toml:17-23 — clippy deny rules mean slice parsing must avoid unwrap/expect/panic patterns.
context: Decode must first check `buffer.len() >= HEADER_LEN`, then parse payload length, then check `buffer.len() >= HEADER_LEN + payload_length` using checked arithmetic.
Unknown frame type is not a decode error: return `Frame::Unknown { type_id, payload, ... }` and consume exactly header plus payload bytes.
Because `payload_length` is `u32`, converting to `usize` and adding to `HEADER_LEN` should use `usize::try_from`/`checked_add` with `ProtocolError::CodecError` on overflow.
Avoid indexing/slicing assumptions that can panic; use `.get(..)`/`.get_mut(..)` and convert absent ranges into `ProtocolError`.
scout.approach: Implement `decode(buffer: &[u8]) -> Result<(Frame, usize), ProtocolError>` as a small state-free parser: validate header length, safely read type/flags/stream/payload length, compute the total frame length with checked arithmetic, validate payload availability, then dispatch on `FrameType::from(type_id)`. For known frame types, parse the deterministic payload layout into typed variants and validate stream IDs through the same helper used by constructors. For `FrameType::Unknown(type_id)`, clone only the payload bytes needed for the returned `Frame::Unknown` and return `Ok((frame, total_len))`.
scout.notes: The brief text says unknown payloads are discarded, but the acceptance says return `Frame::Unknown` with raw payload. Follow the acceptance so tests can assert consumption and byte preservation; callers can discard the returned payload later.

files_changed.note: Public decode() and top-level frame parser.
Private known-frame payload decode helpers by frame category.
Private checked payload reader helpers.

dev.how: Implemented decode(&[u8]) -> Result<(Frame, usize), ProtocolError> with ordered header-length, payload-length, and checked arithmetic validation. Known frames are decoded into typed variants and stream-validated; unknown frame types consume header + payload and return Frame::Unknown with raw payload bytes without disconnecting or erroring.
dev.deviation: Extracted private known-frame and payload reader helpers into codec/known.rs and codec/payload.rs to keep codec.rs below CN1's 500-line limit; semantics match the scouted decode approach.

checklist.note: decode() returns the decoded Frame and exact bytes consumed.
Malformed inputs return ProtocolError variants or CodecError; tests exercise zero-length, partial-header, truncated, invalid-stream, and garbage inputs without panics.
Unknown frame types are length-delimited and returned as Frame::Unknown while consuming the declared payload bytes.

stories.note: SDKs can skip and preserve unknown frame payloads by using the length prefix, supporting additive protocol versions.

acceptance.criterion: decode of a valid encoded frame returns Ok((frame, bytes_consumed)).
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:79-119` returns `(frame, total_len)`; `round_trips_all_named_frame_types` asserts decoded frame equality and consumed length at `codec/tests.rs:16-18`.

acceptance.criterion: decode of a buffer shorter than 10 bytes returns ProtocolError with IncompleteHeader.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:80-84` returns `ProtocolError::IncompleteHeader`; `decode_short_header_returns_incomplete_header` asserts it at `codec/tests.rs:109-115`.

acceptance.criterion: decode of a buffer with complete header but truncated payload returns ProtocolError with TruncatedPayload.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:104-108` returns `ProtocolError::TruncatedPayload`; `decode_truncated_payload_returns_truncated_payload` asserts it at `codec/tests.rs:118-138`.

acceptance.criterion: decode of an unknown frame type returns Frame::Unknown with the raw payload, consuming header + payload bytes.
acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:284-290` returns `Frame::Unknown` with `payload.to_vec()`; `decode_unknown_frame_type_returns_unknown_and_consumes_payload` asserts payload and consumed bytes at `codec/tests.rs:141-156`.

acceptance.criterion: decode never panics for any input (fuzz-safe).
acceptance.evidence: `decode` uses length checks and checked arithmetic at `codec.rs:80-114`; `decode_handles_garbage_inputs_without_panicking` exercises empty, partial, and malformed inputs at `codec/tests.rs:166-180`; grep found no `unwrap`, `expect`, or `panic!` in `crates/liminal/src/protocol`.

requirements.title: Verify round-trip property for all frame types
requirements.spec: FOR every named FrameType variant, decode(encode(frame)) SHALL produce the original frame with byte-identical payloads. This property SHALL be verified by tests covering every frame type. The round-trip guarantee ensures SDK interoperability across languages.
acceptance: A round-trip test exists for every named FrameType variant (20 tests or parameterized).
Each test constructs a frame, encodes to bytes, decodes from bytes, and asserts equality.
Frame variants with payload fields (Publish, ConversationMessage) round-trip with byte-identical payloads.
Frame variants with no payload (Ping, Pong, Disconnect) round-trip correctly.

files: docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:127-144 — round-trip requirement and payload/no-payload coverage expectations.
docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-001.json:155-162 — canonical verification list, including all 20 frame round trips and decode panic-safety cases.
docs/design/protocol/checklist.json:21-29 — checklist C11 is the round-trip property and sits alongside malformed/unknown decode checks.
docs/design/protocol/stories.json:5-10 — S3 frames the SDK interoperability purpose for byte-identical round trips.
Cargo.toml:17-23 — test code must still account for workspace clippy denies on unwrap/expect/panic when run with `--all-targets`.
context: There is no existing test style in `crates/liminal/src` yet, so colocated `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `codec.rs` is the least surprising option for codec property tests.
Round-trip tests should cover all 20 named `FrameType` variants, not just representative frame categories.
Payload-bearing frames need byte-identical payload assertions; no-payload frames such as Ping/Pong/Disconnect need zero-length payload/header-only assertions.
Avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in tests because workspace clippy denies them; use `assert!(result.is_ok())` plus `match`/`let Ok(...) = ... else { ... }` patterns.
scout.approach: Add a parameterized-style test table in `codec.rs` that constructs one valid `Frame` for each named `FrameType`, allocates a test buffer sized from `encoded_len`, calls `encode`, then `decode`, and asserts both equality and exact byte consumption. Include explicit tests for payload bytes on `Publish` and `ConversationMessage`, empty payload/no-payload frames, incomplete header, truncated payload, and unknown type. Keep fixture constructors small and deterministic so a future SDK can copy the expected byte sequences into cross-language tests.
scout.notes: If `Frame` contains `Vec<u8>` and `String` fields, derive `PartialEq, Eq` on all involved types early; otherwise the round-trip tests become noisy. `assert_eq!` is fine, but avoid explicit `panic!`, `.unwrap()`, or `.expect()` in helpers because clippy is configured strictly for all targets.

files_changed.note: Codec test module covering all named frame types and malformed/unknown decode behavior.
Includes cfg(test) test module wiring.

dev.how: Added a parameterized round-trip test suite that constructs one valid Frame for every named FrameType, encodes into an exact-size buffer, decodes it, and asserts equality and exact byte consumption. Added targeted tests for payload-byte preservation, header byte layout, header-only no-payload frames, incomplete headers, truncated payloads, unknown frame types, invalid streams, and panic-safety over garbage inputs.
dev.deviation: Placed tests in a private codec/tests.rs submodule instead of inline in codec.rs to keep codec.rs under CN1's 500-line limit; coverage follows the scouted plan.

checklist.note: decode(encode(frame)) == frame is tested for all 20 named frame types, including payload-bearing and no-payload variants.

stories.note: The round-trip tests provide deterministic SDK-interoperability coverage for every frame category.

acceptance.criterion: A round-trip test exists for every named FrameType variant (20 tests or parameterized).
acceptance.evidence: Parameterized test `protocol::codec::tests::round_trips_all_named_frame_types` at `codec/tests.rs:4-20`; fixtures cover 6 control frames (`codec/tests.rs:203-224`), 4 subscription frames (`226-251`), 3 publish frames (`253-273`), 4 conversation frames (`275-304`), and 3 pressure frames (`306-325`) = 20 named variants.

acceptance.criterion: Each test constructs a frame, encodes to bytes, decodes from bytes, and asserts equality.
acceptance.evidence: `round_trips_all_named_frame_types` constructs each sample, calls `encode`, calls `decode`, and asserts equality at `codec/tests.rs:6-18`; targeted cargo test `cargo test -p liminal round_trips_all_named_frame_types` passed.

acceptance.criterion: Frame variants with payload fields (Publish, ConversationMessage) round-trip with byte-identical payloads.
acceptance.evidence: `payload_bearing_frames_preserve_payload_bytes` constructs Publish and ConversationMessage payloads and asserts payload equality at `codec/tests.rs:45-72`.

acceptance.criterion: Frame variants with no payload (Ping, Pong, Disconnect) round-trip correctly.
acceptance.evidence: `no_payload_frames_round_trip_as_header_only` covers Disconnect, Ping, and Pong at `codec/tests.rs:75-91`, asserting 10-byte header-only encoding and decoded equality.

issues: The original round-trip fixture covered `ConversationClose` only with `Some(reason_code)`, missing the valid `None` optional reason-code case that exposed the R5 encode defect.
fixes: Added `conversation_close_optional_reason_code_round_trips` covering both `None` and `Some(0x0100)` at `crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/tests.rs:93-107`; targeted test passed.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement MessageEnvelope or CausalContext semantics — that is PROTO-002.
SHALL NOT implement connection state machine or handshake logic — that is PROTO-003.
SHALL NOT implement stream table or allocation logic — that is PROTO-004.
SHALL NOT implement backpressure state machine — that is PROTO-005.
SHALL NOT implement schema negotiation logic — that is PROTO-006.
SHALL NOT implement async I/O — the codec is synchronous on byte slices (P4).
SHALL NOT disconnect on unknown frame types — skip them (CN9).
verification: cargo check compiles the protocol module with no errors.
grep -r 'fn ' crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs returns no function definitions.
All 20 FrameType variants have round-trip tests.
decode never panics for zero-length, partial-header, or garbage input.
